pattern: Scanning forward while tracking only the running minimum and maximum value from the window that is already far enough behind turns an all-pairs search into a single linear pass.
hints: You don't need to compare every pair of indices -- as you move forward, only think about the index that is exactly indexDifference behind you and everything before it.
Keep a running record of the smallest and largest value seen so far in that earlier window; a match exists the moment the current value is far enough from either of those two extremes.

Update the running minimum and maximum index as you go, then check both `nums[i] - runningMin` and `runningMax - nums[i]` against valueDifference at every step -- the first index where either check succeeds gives a valid pair immediately.

edge_inputs.description: equal values far enough apart in index, value difference of zero is trivially satisfied
a mix of negative and positive values
a single-element array where an index may pair with itself
a single-element array where the required index gap can never be reached
value difference of zero makes any index pair meeting the gap requirement valid
every value is identical so no positive value difference can ever be met
the value difference exactly equals the maximum possible gap between two values
index difference of zero still requires two distinct positions to supply the value gap

stress.description: large random arrays with random index and value difference thresholds
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    nums = [rng.randint(0, 1000000000) for _ in range(n)]
    indexDifference = rng.randint(0, n - 1) if n > 1 else 0
    valueDifference = rng.randint(0, 1000000000)
    return [nums, indexDifference, valueDifference]
